basis: Static architecture audit for Codex Web controller + root agent separation. This is a source-boundary gate, not a replacement for Go tests or browser visual audits.

checks.name: controller entry lives under backend/cmd/codex-web
checks.details: backend/cmd/codex-web/main.go

checks.name: agent is a root-level Go module, not nested under backend
checks.details: agent/go.mod and agent/main.go exist; backend/cmd/codex-agent must not exist

checks.name: backend internal packages stay controller-only
checks.details: actual=config,model,node,server; missing=none; unexpected=none

checks.name: agent internal packages own transport, host, and session responsibilities
checks.details: actual=agent,config,host,model,session; missing=none; unexpected=none

checks.name: removed backend local Codex/appserver/bridge/host packages
checks.details: backend/internal must not contain local execution packages

checks.name: backend does not execute Codex or import agent internals
checks.details: no forbidden execution references found

checks.name: agent session manager runs Codex CLI turns
checks.details: agent/internal/session/manager.go contains the CLI execution path for new and resumed sessions

checks.name: agent turns are independent of browser request lifetime
checks.details: session turns detach from the controller HTTP request context before running the CLI, with a fake Codex regression test

checks.name: agent owns git execution, backend only forwards git requests
checks.details: git command execution is under agent/internal/host; backend exposes POST /api/git as a remote op

checks.name: controller exposes required API routes
checks.details: backend/internal/server/app.go routes nodes, sessions, workspace, git, and agent websocket endpoints

checks.name: controller session API forwards to agent operations
checks.details: session/workspace/git HTTP handlers use node.Client.Request operations

checks.name: session SSE supports both backlog and live updates
checks.details: GET /api/sessions/events replays backlog for one session and streams agent session.event messages

checks.name: session SSE disables reverse-proxy buffering
checks.details: SSE responses set X-Accel-Buffering: no so nginx can flush stream headers/backlog promptly

checks.name: remote disconnect closes live SSE subscribers
checks.details: Remote.Close resolves pending requests and closes subscribed event streams so browsers can reconnect instead of hanging on a stale node stream

checks.name: remote request write failure tears down the stale node connection
checks.details: controller write failures close the remote, mark the node offline, and close event streams instead of leaving a stale online client

checks.name: session list page can subscribe to all session events
checks.details: omitting sessionId keeps the SSE stream open for every session on the selected node

checks.name: frontend exposes explicit node switching for multi-agent control
checks.details: the Codex panel stores normalized nodes, shows a multi-node selector only when needed, switches active node through the controller, and rebuilds session/SSE state

checks.name: frontend has a controller-owned node management view
checks.details: the workspace can switch from the Codex view to a controller-owned Nodes view, list nodes, select the active node, delete offline nodes, and keep Codex panel node state synchronized

checks.name: frontend has controller-owned workspace and git views
checks.details: the workspace Activity Bar can switch to file browsing and source-control views that call the controller workspace/git APIs for the active agent node

checks.name: frontend has a controller-owned runs view
checks.details: the workspace Activity Bar can switch to a controller-owned Runs view that aggregates online-node sessions, subscribes to live session events, opens a run in the Codex panel, and cancels active turns through the controller

checks.name: node routing never silently falls back to local execution
checks.details: missing, offline, and unknown nodes are explicit errors

checks.name: node id priority includes body, header, and query
checks.details: requestNodeID checks explicit body nodeId, X-Codex-Web-Node-ID, then query nodeId

checks.name: agent websocket protocol has typed envelopes and request correlation
checks.details: backend and agent model envelopes carry type, requestId, nodeId, result, error, and event

checks.name: agent hello, heartbeat, request, response, and event flow is implemented
checks.details: agent connects outbound, registers with hello, heartbeats, handles controller.request, forwards session events, and disconnects unblock pending controller requests

checks.name: agent responses are bound to the websocket connection that received the request
checks.details: request responses and forwarded events use the connection for the current serve loop instead of a mutable global agent connection, and event write failures break the serve loop for reconnect

checks.name: controller persists node records and generated agent token
checks.details: nodes.json and agent-token.txt are data-dir owned; registry keeps offline records and blocks online deletion

checks.name: stale agent disconnect cannot mark a reconnected node offline
checks.details: registry only removes the matching client on MarkOffline and has a regression test for same-node reconnect races

checks.name: agent config requires controller, identity, token, root, Codex home, and Codex binary
checks.details: agent/internal/config/config.go defines the runtime boundary through environment variables

checks.name: build scripts produce both controller and agent binaries
checks.details: build-all builds frontend and delegates to build.sh; build.sh emits codex-web and codex-agent

checks.name: root Go test wrapper covers both separated modules
checks.details: test-go.sh is the root verification entry for the separate backend and agent Go modules, and it prepares embedded frontend dist for fresh clones

checks.name: Docker agent image stays lightweight and proxy-aware
checks.details: agent/Dockerfile uses the proxy Alpine base and starts codex-agent directly

checks.name: Docker agent helper persists data and imports existing Codex auth/session state
checks.details: run-agent-container mounts persistent codex-home/tmp/root, copies auth/config, imports sessions, and keeps detached containers running

checks.name: systemd keeps only the controller on the host
checks.details: systemd services=codex-web.service

checks.name: frontend assets are bundled and cache-busted
checks.details: frontend build emits app/codex-web.css and app/codex-web.js with timestamped URLs, plus a separate fixture bundle for explicit audits

checks.name: fixture assets are not publicly served unless audit mode is enabled
checks.details: app/codex-fixtures.js remains buildable for local audits but the controller hides it by default in production static serving, including normalized path variants

checks.name: static asset misses return 404 while client routes fall back to index
checks.details: missing JS/CSS/assets are explicit 404s; extensionless client routes still receive index.html without a FileServer redirect

checks.name: static directories are never listed
checks.details: static directories return 404/no-store explicitly while the root path still serves index.html

checks.name: app-owned frontend code does not load remote code-server/webview/auth runtime
checks.details: no remote code-server/webview/auth runtime references in app-owned frontend code

checks.name: current web UI has no browser login/auth surface
checks.details: agent token auth is retained for agent websocket only; browser UI has no login/password route

checks.name: production frontend does not fall back to fixture or local fake sessions
checks.details: fixtures remain available only through explicit codexFixture modes; production API failures surface errors instead of sample/local conversations; fixture session creation is delegated to the fixture bundle

checks.name: production frontend bundle excludes fixture sample data
checks.details: main app bundle contains only the explicit fixture loader; fixture definitions, sample data, and fixture session creation live in app/codex-fixtures.js

checks.name: unified panel audit runner covers virtual scrolling
checks.details: long-session virtual scroll coverage is part of the standard panel audit runner, not a manual-only script

checks.name: unified panel audit runner executes the Runs view smoke
checks.details: the controller-owned Runs sidebar is browser-tested for node/session loading, all-session SSE subscription, cancel routing, and opening a run

checks.name: unified panel audit runner executes controller side-view smoke
checks.details: Nodes, Workspace, and Git side views are browser-tested for active-node selection, offline-node deletion, workspace routing, and git routing
